summary: European people with type 1 diabetes do not necessarily follow a high atherogenic diet; available evidence does not support this claim due to standardized dietary guidance emphasizing balance rather than excessive saturated fats or poor nutrition linked to cardiovascular risk. Clinical focus remains on managing lipids through monitored intake and targeted therapies when needed, suggesting routine adherence to an unhealthful, proatherogenic lifestyle isn't established.

Answer choice: B
